items.title: Mapping shutdown patterns across districts
items.author: John Doe
bodyLines: We compiled district-level disruption signals to show how shutdowns cluster in time and geography.
The dataset draws on public reporting, partner alerts, and technical measurements where available. Patterns help advocates brief policymakers with clearer evidence.
This note will expand as we publish the full methodology and interactive maps.

items.title: Why net neutrality still matters here
items.author: John Doe
bodyLines: Net neutrality is not an abstract regulatory debate—it shapes whether local voices can reach audiences without paying tolls to gatekeepers.
Where prioritization is opaque, independent media and small enterprises are first to lose reach during congestion or political pressure.
We argue for simple, enforceable rules and transparency so users—not intermediaries alone—decide what succeeds online.

items.title: A year inside the monitoring room
items.author: John Doe
bodyLines: Our monitoring room runs on rotating shifts, checklists, and a shared log so nothing gets dropped when traffic spikes.
Verification means cross-checking traceroutes, crowd alerts, and regulator notices before we publish a timeline.
This long read walks through three real incidents and what we changed in process afterward.

items.title: Quick update: March incidents
items.author: John Doe
bodyLines: March saw concentrated throttling reports in two regions alongside short DNS anomalies.
We are validating secondary sources and will issue a fuller bulletin next week.

items.title: Notes from the field team
items.author: John Doe
bodyLines: Field notes capture context that charts miss—how outages feel on the ground, and what small businesses do to stay reachable.
These fragments inform our longer reports and training materials.
